blocks.content: The most tense time of the semester for magic school students had arrived—the end-of-term report cards.
Half a year's efforts were condensed into a single sheet of paper.
The cutoff line determining promotion or failure was as terrifying as a guillotine.
Alpheas had received a six-month suspension of his teaching license from the Magic Association.
The penalty would take effect next semester, and for now, Colley, the most senior teacher, was acting as interim headmaster.
Alpheas, who had been weeding the flower beds, straightened up and smiled.
His adorable disciples were approaching.
Shirone, Iruki, Nade—now notorious troublemakers known throughout the school, but that only made their futures more promising.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Hello, Headmaster.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Hoho. I told you not to call me that. I'm just a old man killing time now.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Come on. You'll be headmaster again in half a year.
Alpheas merely laughed.
He was grateful for his students' unwavering support, but it also made him feel undeserving.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: So, how are you holding up? Must be tough with school back in session.
Nade's face immediately fell.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: I'm going insane. I need to score over 80 in every subject left, and it's killing me.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Heh! Trying to move up this time?
The only way to advance from Class Five to Class Four was to score over 80 in every subject—not an average, making it the highest difficulty.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Of course. Isn't that what students are supposed to do?

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: I suppose so. Well, do your best.
Nade looked disappointed, having hoped for encouragement.
But Alpheas's teaching philosophy was firm.
Students weren't racehorses. Left to roam the plains, they'd realize their own worth.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Want some help?
Shirone rolled up his sleeves and approached to help with the weeding.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Hoho, you can't take an old man's only joy. I'll handle the weeds. You go do what you want.
Nade's eyes filled with despair.
She had been counting on weeding as an escape from reality, but that plan was now foiled.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Alright. Take care.
As they turned toward the dormitory, worry lingered on their faces.
The other students were no different.
Since Arcane's attack, the school remained unsettled, and focusing on studies wasn't easy.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Ugh! I'm losing it. I need to study! Study! But I can't focus!

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: After a big event, it's hard to concentrate on anything else. Same after the recital.
At Iruki's words, Shirone sighed deeply.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Still, you're better off than me. I have twice as many subjects to pull up to 80. I don't know if it's possible.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: I've only got math going for me. Humanities are a lost cause. In that sense, Nade's got the advantage.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: What are you talking about? I don't have any strong subjects. You and Shirone can ace practicals, but I'm stuck with the same Spirit Zone as everyone else.
Class Five's practical exams focused more on Spirit Zone application than magic.
With Immortal Function and Servant Syndrome, Shirone and Iruki could rank high in any practical.
Nade had to compete with ordinary Spirit Zones.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: All this thinking is giving me a headache. Let's just take today off and start tomorrow.
Nade's suggestion met with neither agreement nor disagreement.
Even the diligent Shirone remained silent, his motivation clearly flagging.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Huh? Guys, look….
Shirone pointed toward the central park.
Nade and Iruki turned, their eyes widening.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: What? How are they here?
Canis and Arin were wandering the school grounds.
Spotting Shirone's group, they approached.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Hey. Classes over already? The facilities are nice, but you're coddling the kids too much.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: You! What are you plotting now?
Nade blocked their path.
Though they had heard from Sad that the two had been acquitted, she thought that was the end of it.
Or it should have been.
So why were they here?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What's going on? Do you have business here?
Canis ignored the question.
He studied Shirone.
If anyone at the school intrigued him, it wasn't Alpheas or the teachers—it was Shirone.
The first person to defeat him.
Without surpassing him, any growth would be meaningless.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Listen carefully. If we fight again, the result will be the opposite of last time.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: This guy's still delusional. You're a decade away from beating Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Says the one who'd need ten years just to face me.
